title: 1.1: Tests that in6_mactoifaceid converts a MAC address to a modified EUI-64 interface ID when ulbit=0
1.2: Tests that in6_mactoifaceid converts a MAC address to a modified EUI-64 interface ID when ulbit=1
1.3: Tests that in6_mactoifaceid converts a MAC address to a modified EUI-64 interface ID when ulbit is not provided
1.4: Tests that in6_mactoifaceid converts a second, distinct MAC address using default ulbit behavior
1.5: Tests that in6_mactoifaceid converts a second MAC address when ulbit=1
1.6: Tests that in6_mactoifaceid converts a second MAC address when ulbit=0
1.7: Verifies that round-trip conversion using in6_mactoifaceid followed by in6_ifaceidtomac produces a MAC address consistent with the original when ulbit=0
1.8: Verifies that round-trip conversion using in6_mactoifaceid followed by in6_ifaceidtomac produces a MAC address consistent with the original when ulbit=1
1.9: Verifies that round-trip conversion using in6_mactoifaceid followed by in6_ifaceidtomac produces a MAC address consistent with the original when ulbit is not provided
1.10: Verifies that round-trip conversion produces a MAC address consistent with the original for a second MAC using default ulbit behavior
1.11: Verifies that round-trip conversion produces a MAC address consistent with the original for a second MAC when ulbit=1
1.12: Verifies that round-trip conversion produces a MAC address consistent with the original for a second MAC when ulbit=0
1.13: Tests that in6_addrtomac extracts a MAC address from a link-local IPv6 address constructed using in6_mactoifaceid when ulbit=0
1.14: Tests that in6_addrtomac extracts a MAC address from a link-local IPv6 address constructed using in6_mactoifaceid when ulbit=1
1.15: Tests that in6_addrtomac extracts a MAC address from a link-local IPv6 address constructed using in6_mactoifaceid when ulbit is not provided
1.16: Tests that in6_addrtomac extracts a MAC address from a link-local IPv6 address constructed from a second MAC using default ulbit behavior
1.17: Tests that in6_addrtomac extracts a MAC address from a link-local IPv6 address constructed from a second MAC when ulbit=1
1.18: Tests that in6_addrtomac extracts a MAC address from a link-local IPv6 address constructed from a second MAC when ulbit=0
2.1: Places the tests for in6_mactoifaceid direct conversion in test/scapy/layers/inet6.uts
2.2: Places the tests for round-trip MAC conversion in test/scapy/layers/inet6.uts
2.3: Places the tests for in6_addrtomac extraction in test/scapy/layers/inet6.uts
3.1: All tests use the UTscapy framework with '= Test Name' header syntax
3.2: All tests follow the naming pattern '= function_name() description (test N)'
3.3: All tests use single-line boolean assertions without assert keyword
4.1: Follows uppercase expected value format for test case testing direct in6_mactoifaceid output
4.2: Follows lowercase expected value format for test case testing in6_ifaceidtomac output
4.3: Follows 'FE80::' prefix pattern for test case testing in6_addrtomac with link-local addresses
